content:
I Asked an AI Agent to Build and Launch 10 Websites. It Took About 10 Minutes.
One prompt, four tools over MCP, and a coffee. Here is exactly what happened, including the honest timeline.
I gave Claude Code one instruction: create, publish and index ten websites. Then I watched it do the whole thing. The diagram in this article shows the pipeline.
Using MCP, it reached four tools. Namecheap searched, bought and set up ten domains. Totalum generated and deployed all ten sites, live, with hosting and SSL already handled. Google Search Console received the sitemaps so Google could find them. IndexNow pinged the URLs so Bing and other engines picked them up almost immediately.
No copy pasting, no ten separate dashboards, no exporting from one tool to upload into another. One prompt, one loop, ten live sites.
Now the honest part, because the internet loves to exaggerate this. The building, publishing and submitting genuinely took about ten minutes. Ranking did not happen in ten minutes. It showed up over the following weeks as the pages got indexed and started earning traffic, which is exactly how it should work.
The interesting thing is not that I made ten sites. It is that the tedious, painful part of shipping online, the domains, the hosting, the deploy, the indexing, is now something an agent can do end to end while you watch. Disclosure: I work on Totalum, one of the tools in that chain. Would you trust an agent to buy your domains yet? A year ago I would have said no.
